_metadata.note: Verify all practice IDs, point values, and descriptions against the official CMMC 2.0 Model document at https://www.acq.osd.mil/cmmc/ before production use.

practices.title: Authorized Access Control
practices.plain_english: Only authorized people, programs, and devices should be able to access your systems.
practices.discussion: Limit information system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices (including other information systems).

practices.title: Transaction and Function Control
practices.plain_english: Users should only be able to do what their job requires — no more, no less.
practices.discussion: Limit information system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute.

practices.title: Control CUI Flow
practices.plain_english: Control where CUI can go — prevent it from flowing to unauthorized systems or people.
practices.discussion: Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorizations.

practices.title: Separation of Duties
practices.plain_english: No single person should be able to do something harmful on their own — split critical tasks between people.
practices.discussion: Separate the duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity without collusion.

practices.title: Least Privilege
practices.plain_english: Give users only the permissions they need to do their job, and nothing more.
practices.discussion: Employ the principle of least privilege, including for specific security functions and privileged accounts.

practices.title: Non-Privileged Account Use
practices.plain_english: Admins should use regular accounts for day-to-day work, and only switch to admin accounts when needed.
practices.discussion: Use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing non-security functions.

practices.title: Privileged Functions
practices.plain_english: Log and monitor everything that privileged users do on your systems.
practices.discussion: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions and capture the execution of such functions in audit logs.

practices.title: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
practices.plain_english: Lock accounts after a set number of failed login attempts to prevent brute-force attacks.
practices.discussion: Limit unsuccessful logon attempts.

practices.title: Privacy and Security Notices
practices.plain_english: Show users a security warning when they log in so they know the system is monitored.
practices.discussion: Provide privacy and security notices consistent with CUI rules.

practices.title: Session Lock
practices.plain_english: Automatically lock the screen after a period of inactivity, requiring re-authentication.
practices.discussion: Use session lock with pattern-hiding displays after a period of inactivity.

practices.title: Session Termination
practices.plain_english: Automatically end user sessions after a defined period of inactivity.
practices.discussion: Terminate (automatically) a user session after a defined condition.

practices.title: Control Remote Access
practices.plain_english: Monitor and control all remote access to your systems — VPN, RDP, etc.
practices.discussion: Monitor and control remote access sessions.

practices.title: Remote Access Confidentiality
practices.plain_english: Encrypt remote access sessions so data can't be intercepted in transit.
practices.discussion: Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions.

practices.title: Remote Access Routing
practices.plain_english: Route all remote access through a managed access control point — no split tunneling that bypasses your security controls.
practices.discussion: Route remote access via managed access control points.

practices.title: Privileged Remote Access
practices.plain_english: Get explicit approval before allowing privileged remote access — document who can do it and why.
practices.discussion: Authorize remote execution of privileged commands and access to security-relevant information via remote access only for documented operational needs.

practices.title: Wireless Access Authorization
practices.plain_english: Formally authorize wireless connections before allowing them — no rogue or unauthorized Wi-Fi.
practices.discussion: Authorize wireless access prior to allowing such connections.

practices.title: Wireless Access Protection
practices.plain_english: Protect wireless access using authentication and encryption — no open networks.
practices.discussion: Protect wireless access using authentication and encryption.

practices.title: Mobile Device Connection
practices.plain_english: Control how mobile devices connect to your systems and what they can access.
practices.discussion: Control connection of mobile devices.

practices.title: Encrypt CUI on Mobile
practices.plain_english: Encrypt CUI stored on mobile devices and mobile storage media.
practices.discussion: Encrypt CUI on mobile devices and mobile computing platforms.

practices.title: External System Connections
practices.plain_english: Verify and control connections to external systems — including cloud services and contractor networks.
practices.discussion: Verify and authorize connections from external systems.

practices.title: Portable Storage Use
practices.plain_english: Control what portable storage devices (USB drives, external hard drives) can be used with your systems.
practices.discussion: Limit use of portable storage devices on external systems.

practices.title: Control CUI Posted Publicly
practices.plain_english: Before posting anything to a public website, verify it doesn't contain CUI.
practices.discussion: Control CUI posted or processed on publicly accessible information systems.

practices.title: Role-Based Risk Awareness
practices.plain_english: Make sure all employees understand the security risks associated with their specific job activities.
practices.discussion: Ensure that managers, systems administrators, and users of organizational information systems are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities.

practices.title: Role-Based Training
practices.plain_english: Train personnel to carry out their security responsibilities — not just general awareness, but job-specific security training.
practices.discussion: Ensure that organizational personnel are adequately trained to carry out their assigned information security responsibilities.

practices.title: Insider Threat Awareness
practices.plain_english: Train employees to recognize and report insider threats — including how they might unintentionally cause security incidents.
practices.discussion: Provide security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat.

practices.title: System Auditing
practices.plain_english: Create and retain audit logs so you can trace what happened on your systems — who did what and when.
practices.discussion: Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity.

practices.title: User Accountability
practices.plain_english: Make sure every action in your audit logs can be traced back to a specific user.
practices.discussion: Ensure that the actions of individual system users can be uniquely traced to those users.
example_evidence: Audit log samples showing user attribution

practices.title: Review and Update Logged Events
practices.plain_english: Regularly review what your systems are logging to make sure the right events are being captured.
practices.discussion: Review and update logged events.

practices.title: Alert on Audit Logging Failure
practices.plain_english: Get alerted immediately if your logging system stops working — a silent logging failure is a security gap.
practices.discussion: Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure.

practices.title: Audit Log Correlation
practices.plain_english: Use tools to analyze and correlate audit logs across systems to identify suspicious patterns.
practices.discussion: Correlate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes for investigation and response to indications of unlawful, unauthorized, suspicious, or unusual activity.

practices.title: Reduction and Report Generation
practices.plain_english: Use audit reduction tools to generate meaningful reports without losing raw data — make logs useful, not just voluminous.
practices.discussion: Provide audit record reduction and report generation to support on-demand analysis and reporting.

practices.title: Authoritative Time Source
practices.plain_english: Sync all your systems to a common, authoritative time source so audit logs from different systems can be correlated accurately.
practices.discussion: Provide a system capability that compares and synchronizes internal system clocks with an authoritative source.

practices.title: Protect Audit Information
practices.plain_english: Protect your audit logs from unauthorized access, modification, or deletion — logs are only useful if they can be trusted.
practices.discussion: Protect audit information and audit tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.

practices.title: Audit Management
practices.plain_english: Only authorized administrators should be able to manage the audit system — and this should be limited to a small group.
practices.discussion: Limit management of audit logs to a subset of privileged users.
example_evidence: Access control configuration for log systems

practices.title: System Baselining
practices.plain_english: Create and maintain a baseline configuration for all your systems — know what 'normal' looks like.
practices.discussion: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational information systems.

practices.title: Security Configuration Enforcement
practices.plain_english: Establish and enforce secure configuration settings for all hardware and software — don't leave defaults in place.
practices.discussion: Establish and enforce security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems.

practices.title: System Change Control
practices.plain_english: Formally document, review, and approve all changes to your systems before they are made.
practices.discussion: Track, review, approve, and log changes to organizational systems.

practices.title: Security Impact Analysis
practices.plain_english: Before making any system change, analyze what security impact it might have.
practices.discussion: Analyze the security impact of changes prior to implementation.

practices.title: Access Restriction for Change
practices.plain_english: Only authorized people should be able to make changes to your systems — and only within their defined scope.
practices.discussion: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes.

practices.title: Least Functionality
practices.plain_english: Configure systems to provide only the capabilities needed for their purpose — disable everything else.
practices.discussion: Employ the principle of least functionality by configuring organizational systems to provide only essential capabilities.

practices.title: Nonessential Functionality
practices.plain_english: Identify and remove or disable unnecessary features, functions, ports, and services.
practices.discussion: Restrict, disable, or prevent the use of nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services.

practices.title: Application Execution Policy
practices.plain_english: Control which software can run on your systems — use whitelisting or application controls to prevent unauthorized programs.
practices.discussion: Apply deny-by-exception (blacklisting) policy to prevent use of unauthorized software or deny-all, permit-by-exception (whitelisting) policy.

practices.title: User-Installed Software
practices.plain_english: Control and monitor what software users can install themselves — unauthorized software is a major risk vector.
practices.discussion: Control and monitor user-installed software.

practices.title: Identify System Users
practices.plain_english: Every user, process, and device must have a unique identity before being allowed access.
practices.discussion: Identify information system users, processes acting on behalf of users, or devices.

practices.title: Authenticate Users
practices.plain_english: Verify the identity of every user, process, or device before granting access — passwords, MFA, certificates, etc.
practices.discussion: Authenticate (or verify) the identities of those users, processes, or devices, as a prerequisite to allowing access.

practices.title: Multi-Factor Authentication
practices.plain_english: Require at least two forms of verification to access systems with CUI — a password alone is not enough.
practices.discussion: Use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged accounts and network access to non-privileged accounts.

practices.title: Replay-Resistant Authentication
practices.plain_english: Use authentication methods that prevent replay attacks — so stolen credentials can't simply be replayed to gain access.
practices.discussion: Employ replay-resistant authentication mechanisms.

practices.title: Identifier Reuse
practices.plain_english: Don't reuse usernames or account identifiers — each identity should be unique and not recycled.
practices.discussion: Employ identifier management practices that facilitate the tracing of activities to individual users and devices.

practices.title: Identifier Handling
practices.plain_english: Disable accounts after a defined period of inactivity — don't leave dormant accounts that can be exploited.
practices.discussion: Disable identifiers after a defined inactivity period.

practices.title: Password Complexity
practices.plain_english: Enforce minimum password complexity — length, character types, and no common words.
practices.discussion: Enforce a minimum password complexity and change requirements when passwords are used as authenticators.

practices.title: Password Reuse
practices.plain_english: Prevent users from reusing recent passwords — a compromised old password shouldn't be recycled.
practices.discussion: Prohibit password reuse for a specified number of generations.

practices.title: Temporary Passwords
practices.plain_english: Require users to change temporary or initial passwords immediately upon first use.
practices.discussion: Allow temporary password use for system logons with an immediate change to a permanent password.

practices.title: Cryptographically-Protected Passwords
practices.plain_english: Store and transmit passwords in a cryptographically protected form — never in plain text.
practices.discussion: Store and transmit only cryptographically-protected passwords.

practices.title: Obscure Feedback
practices.plain_english: Hide password characters during entry to prevent shoulder surfing.
practices.discussion: Obscure feedback of authentication information.
example_evidence: Application login screen screenshots showing masked input

practices.title: Incident Response Capability
practices.plain_english: Have a documented plan for responding to security incidents — including roles, responsibilities, and communication procedures.
practices.discussion: Establish an operational incident-handling capability for organizational systems that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities.

practices.title: Incident Tracking and Reporting
practices.plain_english: Track and report security incidents to the appropriate authorities — including to the DoD within 72 hours of discovery.
practices.discussion: Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials and/or authorities both internal and external to the organization.

practices.title: Incident Response Testing
practices.plain_english: Regularly test your incident response plan — tabletop exercises, simulations, or actual drills.
practices.discussion: Test the organizational incident response capability.

practices.title: Controlled Maintenance
practices.plain_english: Perform system maintenance in a controlled, scheduled manner — and keep records of what was done.
practices.discussion: Perform maintenance on organizational systems.

practices.title: System Maintenance Control
practices.plain_english: Control all tools and personnel used for system maintenance — including remote maintenance.
practices.discussion: Provide controls on the tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel that conduct system maintenance.

practices.title: Equipment Sanitization
practices.plain_english: Before sending equipment offsite for maintenance, remove or sanitize CUI from it.
practices.discussion: Ensure equipment removed for maintenance is sanitized with respect to any CUI.

practices.title: Media Inspection after Maintenance
practices.plain_english: Check maintenance media (USB drives, diagnostic tools) for malware before using them on your systems.
practices.discussion: Check media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code before the media are used in organizational systems.
example_evidence: Anti-malware scan records for maintenance media

practices.title: Remote Maintenance Sessions
practices.plain_english: Require authentication and monitoring for all remote maintenance sessions — and terminate sessions when complete.
practices.discussion: Require MFA to establish nonlocal maintenance sessions via external network connections.

practices.title: Maintenance Personnel
practices.plain_english: Verify that maintenance personnel are authorized before allowing them to work on your systems.
practices.discussion: Supervise the maintenance activities of maintenance personnel without required access authorization.
example_evidence: Escort procedures for unauthorized maintenance personnel

practices.title: Media Sanitization
practices.plain_english: Before throwing away or reusing storage media, destroy or wipe any Federal Contract Information on it.
practices.discussion: Sanitize or destroy information system media containing Federal Contract Information before disposal or reuse.

practices.title: Media Protection
practices.plain_english: Protect system media — both digital and physical — containing CUI from unauthorized access.
practices.discussion: Protect (i.e., physically control and securely store) system media containing CUI, both paper and digital.

practices.title: Media Access
practices.plain_english: Only authorized personnel should be able to access media containing CUI.
practices.discussion: Limit access to CUI on system media to authorized users.

practices.title: Media Markings
practices.plain_english: Label all media containing CUI so everyone knows it needs to be protected.
practices.discussion: Mark media with necessary CUI markings and distribution limitations.

practices.title: Media Accountability
practices.plain_english: Keep track of all media containing CUI — know where it is at all times.
practices.discussion: Control access to media containing CUI and maintain accountability for media during transport.

practices.title: Portable Storage Encryption
practices.plain_english: Encrypt CUI on portable storage devices like USB drives and external hard drives.
practices.discussion: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of CUI during transport unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards.

practices.title: Removable Media Control
practices.plain_english: Control the use of removable media on your systems — and prohibit it when there is no identifiable operational need.
practices.discussion: Control the use of removable media on system components.

practices.title: Shared Media
practices.plain_english: Before reusing media that held CUI, wipe it so the previous contents cannot be recovered.
practices.discussion: Prohibit the use of portable storage devices when such devices have no identifiable owner.

practices.title: Protect Backups
practices.plain_english: Protect backup copies of CUI — both where they are stored and how they are transmitted.
practices.discussion: Protect the confidentiality of backup CUI at storage locations.

practices.title: Screen Individuals
practices.plain_english: Screen individuals before giving them access to systems that contain CUI — background checks, vetting, etc.
practices.discussion: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access to organizational systems containing CUI.

practices.title: Personnel Termination
practices.plain_english: When an employee leaves, immediately revoke their access to all systems containing CUI.
practices.discussion: Ensure that organizational systems containing CUI are protected during and after personnel actions such as terminations and transfers.

practices.title: Limit Physical Access
practices.plain_english: Only authorized people should be allowed physical access to your systems, equipment, and work areas.
practices.discussion: Limit physical access to organizational information systems, equipment, and the respective operating environments to authorized individuals.

practices.title: Escort Visitors
practices.plain_english: Escort visitors at all times and log their activity when they are in areas with your systems.
practices.discussion: Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity.

practices.title: Physical Access Logs
practices.plain_english: Keep logs of who enters and exits your facilities — especially areas where CUI systems are located.
practices.discussion: Maintain audit logs of physical access.

practices.title: Manage Physical Access Devices
practices.plain_english: Manage and control physical access devices like badges, keys, and combinations — including when employees leave.
practices.discussion: Control and manage physical access devices.

practices.title: Monitor Facility
practices.plain_english: Monitor and control your physical facilities where CUI systems are located — cameras, guards, sensors.
practices.discussion: Protect and monitor the physical facility and support infrastructure for organizational systems.

practices.title: Alternative Work Sites
practices.plain_english: Implement security controls for employees working from home or at alternative locations where CUI may be accessed.
practices.discussion: Enforce safeguarding measures for CUI at alternate work sites.

practices.title: Risk Assessments
practices.plain_english: Periodically assess the risks to your systems and CUI — understand the threats you face and how vulnerable you are.
practices.discussion: Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations, assets, and individuals from the operation of organizational systems and the associated processing, storage, or transmission of CUI.

practices.title: Vulnerability Scanning
practices.plain_english: Regularly scan your systems for known vulnerabilities and remediate them promptly.
practices.discussion: Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities affecting those systems are identified.

practices.title: Vulnerability Remediation
practices.plain_english: Fix the vulnerabilities you find — have a process for prioritizing and remediating them based on risk.
practices.discussion: Remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments.

practices.title: Security Control Assessment
practices.plain_english: Periodically assess your security controls to see if they are working as intended.
practices.discussion: Periodically assess the security controls in organizational systems to determine if the controls are effective in their application.

practices.title: Plan of Action
practices.plain_english: Develop and maintain a POA&M to track and remediate security deficiencies.
practices.discussion: Develop and implement plans of action designed to correct deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities in organizational systems.

practices.title: Security Control Monitoring
practices.plain_english: Continuously monitor your security controls to ensure they remain effective over time.
practices.discussion: Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis to ensure the continued effectiveness of the controls.

practices.title: System Security Plan
practices.plain_english: Develop and maintain a System Security Plan (SSP) that documents how your security controls are implemented.
practices.discussion: Develop, document, and periodically update system security plans that describe system boundaries, system environments of operation, how security requirements are implemented.

practices.title: Boundary Protection
practices.plain_english: Monitor and control network traffic at your system's external and key internal boundaries — firewalls, gateways, etc.
practices.discussion: Monitor, control, and protect organizational communications at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries of the information systems.

practices.title: Public-Access System Separation
practices.plain_english: Physically or logically separate publicly accessible systems from your internal network that handles FCI/CUI.
practices.discussion: Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are physically or logically separated from internal networks.

practices.title: Security Engineering
practices.plain_english: Apply security engineering principles when designing, developing, and modifying your systems.
practices.discussion: Employ architectural designs, software development techniques, and systems engineering principles that promote effective information security.

practices.title: Role Separation
practices.plain_english: Separate user functionality from system management functionality — don't mix admin tools with regular user tools.
practices.discussion: Separate user functionality from system management functionality.

practices.title: Shared Resource Control
practices.plain_english: Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer between systems sharing resources.
practices.discussion: Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.

practices.title: Network Communication by Exception
practices.plain_english: Deny all network traffic by default and only permit what is explicitly authorized — not the other way around.
practices.discussion: Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception (i.e., deny all, permit by exception).

practices.title: Split Tunneling
practices.plain_english: Prevent VPN split tunneling that allows remote workers to access both your secure network and the public internet simultaneously.
practices.discussion: Prevent remote devices from simultaneously connecting with the system using split tunneling mechanisms.

practices.title: Data in Transit
practices.plain_english: Encrypt CUI when it is transmitted across networks — email, file transfers, APIs, everything.
practices.discussion: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards.

practices.title: Terminate Network Connections
practices.plain_english: Terminate network connections after a defined period of inactivity or after session completion.
practices.discussion: Terminate network connections associated with communications sessions at the end of the sessions or after a defined period of inactivity.

practices.title: Key Management
practices.plain_english: Manage cryptographic keys in a secure, controlled manner — generate, store, and retire keys properly.
practices.discussion: Establish and manage cryptographic keys for cryptography employed in organizational systems.

practices.title: CUI Encryption
practices.plain_english: Encrypt CUI at rest — wherever it is stored on your systems.
practices.discussion: Employ FIPS-validated cryptography when used to protect the confidentiality of CUI.

practices.title: Collaborative Computing Devices
practices.plain_english: Control and monitor collaborative devices (cameras, microphones, screen sharing) in spaces where CUI is discussed.
practices.discussion: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices and provide indication of use to present users.

practices.title: Mobile Code
practices.plain_english: Control and monitor the use of mobile code (scripts, active content, applets) in your systems.
practices.discussion: Control and monitor the use of mobile code.

practices.title: VoIP
practices.plain_english: Control and monitor the use of Voice over IP (VoIP) technologies to protect against interception or misuse.
practices.discussion: Control and monitor the use of Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies.

practices.title: Communications Authenticity
practices.plain_english: Use cryptographic or other means to verify that network communications are legitimate and not tampered with.
practices.discussion: Protect the authenticity of communications sessions.

practices.title: Data at Rest
practices.plain_english: Protect CUI at rest — implement appropriate controls for data stored on your systems.
practices.discussion: Protect the confidentiality of CUI at rest.

practices.title: Flaw Remediation
practices.plain_english: Identify, report, and fix security flaws in your systems in a timely manner — don't leave known vulnerabilities unpatched.
practices.discussion: Identify, report, and correct information and information system flaws in a timely manner.

practices.title: Malicious Code Protection
practices.plain_english: Deploy anti-malware protection at appropriate locations across your systems.
practices.discussion: Provide protection from malicious code at appropriate locations within organizational information systems.

practices.title: Update Malicious Code Protection
practices.plain_english: Keep your anti-malware tools and signatures up to date so they can detect the latest threats.
practices.discussion: Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available.
example_evidence: Anti-malware version and signature date records

practices.title: System and File Scanning
practices.plain_english: Perform regular system scans and scan files from external sources (downloads, email attachments) in real time.
practices.discussion: Perform periodic scans of the information system and real-time scans of files from external sources as files are downloaded, opened, or executed.

practices.title: Security Alerts and Advisories
practices.plain_english: Receive, monitor, and respond to security alerts and advisories from vendors and government sources.
practices.discussion: Monitor system security alerts and advisories and take action in response.

practices.title: Security Monitoring
practices.plain_english: Monitor your systems to detect attacks, unauthorized access, and indicators of compromise.
practices.discussion: Monitor the organizational systems, including inbound and outbound communications traffic, to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

practices.title: Identify Unauthorized Use
practices.plain_english: Identify unauthorized use of your systems — detect anomalous behavior that might indicate a breach.
practices.discussion: Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems.
